# OS image factories: bake a bootable image with debos 🟡

**Goal.** Assemble a whole bootable OS image from a declarative recipe with debos (the Debian image builder), then meet the distro wrapper — Kali's kali-vm — built on the very same engine. Learn the tool, then the thing built on it.

**Audience:** you've turned a rootfs into a bootable VM by hand — now do it declaratively  
**Effort:** ⏱ ~1–2h  
**Prerequisites:** [Chroot → running system: the cross-phase bridges](path-chroot-to-system.md)

| # | Lab | You'll learn | Checkpoint (observable) |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | [`debian-vm-builder`](../debian-vm-builder/) | debos directly: a YAML recipe (debootstrap → apt → image-partition → filesystem-deploy → systemd-boot) assembled inside a fakemachine KVM build VM — no installer, no by-hand loop-mounting. | `build-debian-vm.sh` bakes a trixie qcow2; `run-graphical.sh` boots it under OVMF to a `debian-vm login:` (SSH `debian`/`debian`). |
| 2 | [`kali-vm-builder`](../kali-vm-builder/) 🔒 | Kali's official `kali-vm` is a thin wrapper around the SAME debos engine, producing the image you'd otherwise download — now the wrapper reads clearly, because you saw the machinery underneath. | `build-kali-vm.sh --headless` builds a QCOW2; `run-graphical.sh` boots it to a Kali login. |
